item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, though I wonder if they're adapted to living in darkness. There's no natural light, so if SCP-3427-1D stops working, they're left in the dark.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The person being interviewed in your interview log doesn't sound like a random person from the society inside this body, to me. They just sound like another Foundation researcher. Still, I liked this enough to give it an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are already two skips about people who are countries, and nothing about this seemed particularly original in comparison. Also, you didn't capitalize "Foundation" in the first paragraph of the description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On its own, the article is well done and enjoyable. However, the fact that we've got 007 already has left me at a no-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for originality, although you need to fix the interview log. The second to last entry, I don't know who's speaking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this idea and I think the execution was done quite well. It's detail may be a bit too much to some people but to me it was just enough to not be a complete chore to read.

SCP-3427-1B is the largest colony within SCP-3427-1B

Although this line kinda confuses me. How is the colony the largest in the colony?

+1
